samples.prompt: Analyze this Boeing 747-400 commercial airliner. Describe the aircraft's wing configuration, engine placement, and identify the specific variant features visible in this image.
samples.text: The Boeing 747-400 is a wide-body, four-engine jet airliner. It features a distinctive upper deck hump, high-bypass turbofan engines mounted on pylons under swept wings, and a conventional tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer.
samples.vfa_target: Aircraft aerodynamics and commercial aviation engineering

questions.text: What type of engines does the Boeing 747-400 typically use?
What is the typical cruise altitude for a Boeing 747-400?

samples.prompt: Examine this Airbus A380. What aerodynamic features are visible? Describe the wing design, engine configuration, and how this double-decker aircraft achieves its lift characteristics.
samples.text: The Airbus A380 is a full-length double-deck, wide-body, four-engine jet. It features swept wings with a high aspect ratio, winglets for fuel efficiency, and four large turbofan engines. The aircraft's massive size requires reinforced landing gear with 22 wheels.
samples.vfa_target: Large aircraft aerodynamics and structural engineering

questions.text: How many wheels does the Airbus A380 landing gear have?
What is the wingspan of the Airbus A380?

samples.prompt: Analyze this Space Shuttle. Describe the thermal protection system, solid rocket boosters, and how the vehicle transitions from launch to orbital flight to reentry.
samples.text: The Space Shuttle was a partially reusable low Earth orbital spacecraft system. It featured thermal protection tiles, two solid rocket boosters, an external tank, and three main engines. The vehicle launched vertically and landed horizontally as a glider.
samples.vfa_target: Spacecraft engineering and orbital mechanics

questions.text: What material were the Space Shuttle's thermal protection tiles made of?
How many Space Shuttle missions were flown in total?

samples.prompt: Examine this Boeing CH-47 Chinook helicopter. Describe its tandem rotor configuration, the advantages of this design, and how it achieves vertical lift and directional control.
samples.text: The Boeing CH-47 Chinook is a tandem-rotor, heavy-lift helicopter. Its dual rotor configuration eliminates the need for a tail rotor, providing superior lifting capacity and stability. The counter-rotating rotors cancel out torque effects.
samples.vfa_target: Helicopter aerodynamics and rotorcraft engineering

questions.text: What is the main advantage of tandem rotor configuration?
What is the maximum payload capacity of the CH-47 Chinook?

samples.prompt: Analyze this Gulfstream G650 business jet. Describe its swept wing design, engine configuration, and how the aircraft achieves its long-range cruise efficiency at high altitude.
samples.text: The Gulfstream G650 is a large-cabin, ultra-long-range business jet. It features swept wings with winglets, two Rolls-Royce BR725 turbofan engines, and a fly-by-wire flight control system. The aircraft cruises at Mach 0.85-0.90 at altitudes up to 51,000 feet.
samples.vfa_target: Business aviation and long-range aircraft design

questions.text: What is the maximum cruise speed of the Gulfstream G650?
What engines power the Gulfstream G650?

samples.prompt: Analyze this container cargo ship. Describe its hull design, propulsion system, and how modern container ships achieve fuel efficiency while carrying thousands of TEU containers.
samples.text: Modern container cargo ships feature bulbous bows for reduced hydrodynamic resistance, large diesel engines for propulsion, and stacked container arrangements above and below deck. These vessels can carry 20,000+ TEU containers across oceans.
samples.vfa_target: Marine engineering and naval architecture

questions.text: What does TEU stand for in container shipping?
What is the purpose of a bulbous bow on cargo ships?

samples.prompt: Examine this Caterpillar 797F mining haul truck. Describe its massive size, suspension system, and how it operates in open-pit mining environments to transport ore and overburden.
samples.text: The Caterpillar 797F is one of the world's largest mining haul trucks. It features a payload capacity of 400 tons, hydraulic suspension, and a diesel-electric powertrain. The truck operates in extreme conditions in open-pit mines worldwide.
samples.vfa_target: Heavy equipment engineering and mining operations

questions.text: What is the payload capacity of the Caterpillar 797F?
What type of powertrain does the 797F use?

samples.prompt: Analyze this tower crane at a construction site. Describe its jib configuration, counterweight system, and how it lifts heavy loads to significant heights while maintaining stability.
samples.text: Tower cranes feature a horizontal jib, counter-jib with counterweights, and a vertical tower structure. They use a trolley mechanism to move loads along the jib and can lift loads of 20+ tons to heights exceeding 200 meters.
samples.vfa_target: Structural engineering and construction equipment

questions.text: What provides stability for a tower crane?
What mechanism moves loads horizontally on a tower crane?

samples.prompt: Examine this hydraulic excavator. Describe its boom, arm, and bucket configuration, hydraulic system, and how it achieves precise digging and material handling in construction and mining applications.
samples.text: Hydraulic excavators feature a boom, arm, and bucket connected by hydraulic cylinders. The hydraulic system provides the force needed for digging, while the operator controls precise movements through joysticks and pedals.
samples.vfa_target: Hydraulic systems and construction equipment engineering

questions.text: What provides the digging force in a hydraulic excavator?
What is the typical operating weight of a large mining excavator?

samples.prompt: Analyze this heavy haul truck used for oversized cargo transport. Describe its multi-axle configuration, steering system, and how it manages weight distribution for transporting extremely heavy loads.
samples.text: Heavy haul trucks feature multiple axles for weight distribution, hydraulic steering systems, and powerful diesel engines. These vehicles transport oversized cargo like transformers, generators, and industrial equipment.
samples.vfa_target: Transportation engineering and load management

questions.text: Why do heavy haul trucks have multiple axles?
What type of loads do heavy haul trucks typically transport?

samples.prompt: Analyze this industrial robot arm in a manufacturing setting. Describe its joint configuration, degrees of freedom, and how it achieves precise positioning for assembly tasks.
samples.text: Industrial robot arms typically feature 6 degrees of freedom, allowing them to reach any position and orientation in their workspace. They use servo motors and encoders for precise positioning and can perform tasks like welding, painting, and assembly.
samples.vfa_target: Robotics engineering and automation systems

questions.text: How many degrees of freedom does a typical industrial robot arm have?
What sensors provide position feedback in robot arms?

samples.prompt: Examine this autonomous mobile robot in a warehouse setting. Describe its navigation system, sensor suite, and how it navigates dynamic environments while avoiding obstacles.
samples.text: Autonomous mobile robots use LiDAR, cameras, and ultrasonic sensors for navigation. They employ simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) algorithms to build maps and track their position in real-time.
samples.vfa_target: Mobile robotics and autonomous navigation

questions.text: What does SLAM stand for in robotics?
What primary sensor do autonomous robots use for obstacle detection?

samples.prompt: Analyze this collaborative robot (cobot) working alongside human operators. Describe its safety features, force-limited joints, and how it achieves safe human-robot interaction.
samples.text: Collaborative robots feature force-limited joints, rounded edges, and proximity sensors to ensure safe human-robot interaction. They can stop or yield when detecting contact, making them suitable for shared workspaces.
samples.vfa_target: Human-robot interaction and collaborative automation

questions.text: What is the main safety feature of collaborative robots?
What distinguishes cobots from traditional industrial robots?

samples.prompt: Analyze this CNC machining center. Describe its tool changer system, spindle configuration, and how computer numerical control achieves precise material removal in manufacturing.
samples.text: CNC machining centers feature automatic tool changers, high-speed spindles, and computer-controlled axes. They read G-code instructions to precisely remove material from workpieces, achieving tolerances of ±0.001 inches.
samples.vfa_target: Precision manufacturing and CNC technology

questions.text: What language do CNC machines use for programming?
What is the typical tolerance achievable with CNC machining?

samples.prompt: Examine this industrial 3D printer. Describe its additive manufacturing process, build chamber, and how it creates complex geometries layer by layer using various materials.
samples.text: Industrial 3D printers use additive manufacturing to build objects layer by layer. They can process metals, plastics, and ceramics, creating complex geometries impossible with traditional manufacturing methods.
samples.vfa_target: Additive manufacturing and rapid prototyping

questions.text: What is the fundamental principle of 3D printing?
Which material can industrial 3D printers process?

samples.prompt: Analyze this automated assembly line in an automotive factory. Describe the conveyor system, robotic workstations, and how the line achieves high throughput with consistent quality.
samples.text: Automated assembly lines use conveyors to move products through sequential workstations. Robots perform repetitive tasks like welding and fastening, while quality control stations ensure consistency.
samples.vfa_target: Production engineering and automation

questions.text: What is the primary benefit of assembly line production?
Who pioneered the moving assembly line?

samples.prompt: Analyze this offshore oil platform in the North Sea. Describe its structural design, drilling equipment, and how it withstands harsh marine conditions while extracting hydrocarbons.
samples.text: Offshore oil platforms feature jacket structures anchored to the seabed, drilling derricks, and processing facilities. They withstand extreme waves, winds, and currents while extracting oil and gas from subsea reservoirs.
samples.vfa_target: Offshore engineering and petroleum extraction

questions.text: What type of structure is commonly used for offshore platforms in shallow water?
What is the maximum depth for fixed offshore platforms?

samples.prompt: Examine this modern wind turbine. Describe its rotor design, gearbox system, and how it converts wind energy into electrical power through electromagnetic induction.
samples.text: Modern wind turbines feature three-blade rotors connected to gearboxes and generators. The gearbox increases rotational speed from 10-20 RPM to 1,500-1,800 RPM for the generator.
samples.vfa_target: Renewable energy engineering and wind power

questions.text: How many blades do modern wind turbines typically have?
What does the gearbox do in a wind turbine?

samples.prompt: Analyze this utility-scale solar photovoltaic farm. Describe the panel configuration, tracking systems, and how it converts sunlight into grid-compatible electrical power.
samples.text: Utility-scale solar farms use thousands of photovoltaic panels mounted on tracking systems. Single-axis trackers follow the sun's east-west movement, increasing energy yield by 25-30% compared to fixed installations.
samples.vfa_target: Solar energy engineering and grid integration

questions.text: What do solar tracking systems do?
How much more energy do tracking systems produce vs fixed panels?

samples.prompt: Analyze this printed circuit board (PCB). Describe its layer stackup, component placement, and how surface mount technology enables high-density electronic assembly.
samples.text: Modern PCBs feature multiple layers of copper traces, surface mount components, and via connections between layers. SMT allows components as small as 0201 packages (0.6mm x 0.3mm) to be placed automatically.
samples.vfa_target: Electronic packaging and PCB design

questions.text: What does SMT stand for in electronics manufacturing?
What is the purpose of vias in a PCB?

samples.prompt: Examine this semiconductor silicon wafer. Describe the photolithography process, transistor structures, and how billions of transistors are fabricated on a single chip.
samples.text: Semiconductor fabrication uses photolithography to pattern transistors on silicon wafers. Modern processes achieve feature sizes below 7nm, allowing billions of transistors on a single chip.
samples.vfa_target: Semiconductor fabrication and nanotechnology

questions.text: What process patterns transistors on silicon wafers?
What is the smallest feature size in modern chip fabrication?

samples.prompt: Analyze this advanced semiconductor package. Describe the lead frame, wire bonding, and how the package provides electrical connections and thermal management for the die.
samples.text: Semiconductor packages protect the die and provide electrical connections to the PCB. Wire bonds or flip-chip bumps connect the die to the lead frame, while thermal pads dissipate heat.
samples.vfa_target: Electronic packaging and thermal management

questions.text: What connects the die to the lead frame in wire bonding?
What is the main purpose of semiconductor packaging?

samples.prompt: Analyze this Boeing 747 during a nose-up climb. Describe the aircraft's pitch angle, wing loading during climb, and how the high-lift devices (flaps and slats) are configured for this climb attitude.
samples.text: During a nose-up climb, the Boeing 747 increases its angle of attack, generating additional lift from the wing's camber. The leading-edge slats and trailing-edge flaps may be partially deployed at lower altitudes. The four turbofan engines provide excess thrust to maintain the climb rate while the fuselage attitude tilts upward relative to the horizon.
samples.vfa_target: Aircraft climb dynamics and high-lift aerodynamics
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent upward pitch angle, fuselage oscillates or drifts level mid-generation

questions.text: What is the typical climb angle for a Boeing 747 after takeoff?
What happens to induced drag during a steep climb?
Which control surface primarily controls pitch attitude?
What is the relationship between angle of attack and lift in the linear range?

samples.prompt: Analyze this offshore wind farm from a vertical rise perspective. Describe the turbine spacing, foundation types, and how the array layout optimizes energy capture while minimizing wake interference between turbines.
samples.text: Offshore wind farms use monopile or jacket foundations anchored to the seabed. Turbines are spaced 5-9 rotor diameters apart to minimize wake losses. The vertical rise viewpoint reveals the staggered array layout designed to maximize energy capture from prevailing wind directions while reducing turbulence from upstream turbines.
samples.vfa_target: Offshore wind farm layout and wake interaction dynamics
samples.failure_target: I2V model distorts turbine vertical proportions during upward camera rise, towers appear to stretch or compress unevenly

questions.text: What is the typical spacing between offshore wind turbines?
What is a monopile foundation used for in offshore wind?
What is wake interference in a wind farm?

samples.prompt: Analyze this conveyor belt system from a side view. Describe the belt material, roller configuration, drive mechanism, and how the system maintains consistent tension and speed for material transport.
samples.text: Industrial conveyor belts use vulcanized rubber or PVC belts supported by idler rollers spaced at regular intervals. The drive pulley at the head provides traction through friction, while gravity take-up mechanisms maintain belt tension. Speed is controlled by variable frequency drives (VFDs) connected to the motor and gearbox assembly.
samples.vfa_target: Conveyor kinematics and material handling dynamics
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to render consistent belt motion, rollers appear to rotate out of sync or belt surface texture shifts during translation

questions.text: What maintains tension in a conveyor belt system?
What device controls conveyor belt speed?
What provides traction between the drive pulley and belt?
What is the typical spacing of idler rollers on a conveyor?

samples.prompt: Analyze this assembly line with a robotic arm performing kinematic operations. Describe the robot's joint movements, end-effector configuration, and how the arm coordinates with the moving assembly line to perform tasks on parts in motion.
samples.text: Assembly line robotic arms use synchronized motion control to track parts on a moving conveyor. The robot's trajectory planner computes inverse kinematics solutions in real-time, adjusting joint angles to maintain the end-effector position relative to the moving workpiece. Force-torque sensors at the wrist provide feedback for precise assembly operations.
samples.vfa_target: Robotic kinematics and synchronized assembly automation
samples.failure_target: I2V model desynchronizes robot arm motion from conveyor speed, end-effector drifts away from workpiece during generation

questions.text: What does inverse kinematics compute in robotics?
What sensor provides force feedback at a robot wrist?
How do robots track parts on a moving conveyor?

samples.prompt: Analyze this PCB during a dolly-in zoom toward a specific component cluster. Describe the surface mount components, solder joint geometry, trace routing, and how the zoom reveals progressively finer details of the circuit board construction.
samples.text: A dolly-in zoom on a PCB reveals layers of detail: from the macro layout of component placement and trace routing, through individual SMD packages with their pad geometries, down to solder fillet profiles and via structures. The depth of field narrows as the camera approaches, isolating the target components from the surrounding board.
samples.vfa_target: PCB macro-to-micro structural detail and component inspection
samples.failure_target: I2V model loses fine detail during dolly-in zoom, solder joints blur or trace patterns become inconsistent at closer viewpoints

questions.text: What happens to depth of field during a dolly-in zoom?
What is the smallest common SMD package size?
What connects traces between PCB layers?
What is a solder fillet?

samples.prompt: Analyze this heavy truck engine bay during a dolly-in zoom. Describe the diesel engine components, cooling system layout, turbocharger placement, and how the progressive zoom reveals the mechanical complexity of the powertrain.
samples.text: A dolly-in zoom on a heavy truck engine bay reveals the turbocharged diesel engine with its inline configuration, charge air cooler, and serpentine belt system. The progressive approach exposes details from the overall engine layout to individual fuel injector lines, wiring harnesses, and the turbocharger compressor housing.
samples.vfa_target: Heavy vehicle powertrain architecture and engine component detail
samples.failure_target: I2V model introduces spatial distortion during dolly-in, engine components shift relative positions or turbocharger geometry distorts

questions.text: What is the purpose of a charge air cooler in a diesel engine?
What drives the turbocharger in a diesel engine?
What is the typical configuration of heavy truck diesel engines?

samples.prompt: Analyze this wind turbine with spinning blades. Describe the blade pitch control, rotational dynamics, and how the generator converts the mechanical rotation into electrical power through the gearbox and generator assembly.
samples.text: Wind turbine blades rotate at 10-20 RPM, with pitch actuators adjusting blade angle to optimize energy capture. The low-speed shaft drives through a planetary gearbox to the high-speed generator shaft. Yaw motors orient the nacelle into the wind, while blade pitch and generator torque are continuously adjusted by the turbine controller.
samples.vfa_target: Wind turbine rotational dynamics and power generation kinematics
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent blade rotation speed, blades appear to stutter or change angular velocity mid-spin

questions.text: What is the typical rotational speed of a wind turbine rotor?
What controls blade angle on a wind turbine?
What converts low-speed rotation to high-speed rotation in a turbine?
What orients the turbine nacelle into the wind?

samples.prompt: Analyze this FANUC robotic arm from a 45-degree orbital camera path. Describe the robot's mechanical structure, joint configuration, and how the orbital viewpoint reveals the workspace envelope and reach capabilities of the industrial manipulator.
samples.text: A FANUC industrial robot arm viewed from a 45-degree orbital path reveals its six-axis articulated structure: base rotation, shoulder and elbow pitch, and three wrist axes (roll, pitch, yaw). The orbital camera path exposes the full workspace envelope, showing how the arm can reach positions above, below, and around the work cell from its fixed base.
samples.vfa_target: Industrial robot spatial geometry and workspace kinematics
samples.failure_target: I2V model distorts robot link proportions during orbit, joint positions shift inconsistently or arm geometry warps at oblique angles

questions.text: How many axes does a typical FANUC industrial robot have?
What is the workspace envelope of a robot arm?
options: The volume the end-effector can reach

questions.text: What type of joint provides base rotation on a FANUC robot?
